nodes.norm_label: permissionrule (kubernetes rbac rule model in permissionset)

nodes.label: SealedCausalChain lineage spec field (immutable after creation)
nodes.rationale: Lineage is a sealed spec field (not annotation) so it becomes a contract, not advisory. Admission webhook rejects any update modifying the lineage field after creation, enforcing an immutable causal chain for every root declaration. This is Decision 1.
nodes.norm_label: sealedcausalchain lineage spec field (immutable after creation)

nodes.label: CreationRationale controlled vocabulary (ClusterProvision, SecurityEnforcement, etc.)
nodes.rationale: Rationale values are a compile-time enumeration owned by Seam Core rationale.go, not free-text. New values require a PR to seam-core and Governor review. Operators import, never extend unilaterally. This is Decision 5.

nodes.label: ONT: Operator Native Thinking principle
nodes.rationale: Infrastructure governance is the consequence of living documentation, not the purpose. The cluster is the documentation itself — not a representation of it. Every CRD is the intent, every operator is the runbook running.

nodes.label: Five-layer Living Documentation Architecture
nodes.rationale: Architecture layers: Component Deploy -> LineageController -> Lineage Sink -> Document Store (Neo4j/PostgreSQL + MongoDB) -> Translation (NLP fills schema slots). AI narrates only; it never decides, infers relationships, or overrides human-set flags.

nodes.label: Guardian Admission Webhook
nodes.rationale: Admission webhook is the enforcement mechanism for all RBAC governance. RBACProfiles exceeding their governing RBACPolicy are rejected at admission. The webhook also enforces lineage field immutability after object creation.
